data.job_title: Accountant (5 years Indian accounting expereince, AP, AR, GL, Pune based)
data.job_description:
Wolters Kluwer is a global leader in professional information, software solutions, and services for the

health, tax & accounting, governance, risk & compliance, and legal & regulatory sectors. Wolters Kluwer is

headquartered in Alphen aan den Rijn, the Netherlands. The Company serves customers in over 180

countries, maintains operations in over 40 countries, and employs approximately 19,000 people worldwide.

Every day, our customers make critical decisions to help save lives, improve the way they do business,

build better judicial and regulatory systems. We help them get it right!

The position will support the management by ensuring all the accounting activities are managed accurately

and in a timely manner maintaining the integrity of financial data. This position is critical to the overall

control environment for the divisions supported by Wolters Kluwer Financial Shared Services (FSS). FSS

focuses on building accounting expertise in a tower structure: Record to Report, Procure to Pay and Order

to Cash and provides support to the businesses of Wolters Kluwer in a standardized, harmonized, and high

quality manner. If successful, the candidate will be able to expand his/her expertise within a global

organization. Additional requirements include, but are not limited to:
• Good communication and interpersonal skills to establish strong relationships with cross-

functional teams.
• An ability to quickly adapt to changes in business requirements.
• A desire to learn new skills and expand areas of responsibility

Essential Duties And Responsibilities
• Actively participate in the month end close in accordance with the Corporate Reporting calendar
• Ensuring alignment between bank GL accounts and bank/ PG provider statements
• Ensure timely booking of international remittances at Consolidated level
• Managing inter company entries
• Managing loads into STEP SAP
• Managing AP activities by ensuring correct flow of entries from Coupa Standard to STEP SAP. Ensure

vendors are paid on timely basis.
• Manage statutory payments.
• Cash applications
• Manage accruals/JEs
• Manage employee reimbursement.
• Manage Fixed Asset accounting
• Manage communications with FP&A team and relevant other stakeholders
• Maintain and update desk-top procedures for any changes in processes, tools and procedures
• Keep updated checklists and ensure completion of tasks per such checklists
• Prepare supporting schedules and analyses as required by management
• Timely and effectively communicate with accounting, finance and operations teams by providing thorough and

thoughtful responses to inquiries and questions
• Any other assigned work as appropriate

Other Duties

Special projects as appropriate

Job Qualifications

Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting
• 3-5 years of previous accounting/audit experience in public or private accounting
• Experience with SAP and/or other ERPs strongly preferred
• Experience with Blackline strongly preferred
• Strong reporting and data querying capabilities
• Thorough knowledge of MS Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Access)

Our Interview Practices

To maintain a fair and genuine hiring process, we kindly ask that all candidates participate in interviews without the assistance of AI tools or external prompts. Our interview process is designed to assess your individual skills, experiences, and communication style. We value authenticity and want to ensure we’re getting to know you—not a digital assistant. To help maintain this integrity, we ask to remove virtual backgrounds and include in-person interviews in our hiring process. Please note that use of AI-generated responses or third-party support during interviews will be grounds for disqualification from the recruitment process.

Applicants may be required to appear onsite at a Wolters Kluwer office as part of the recruitment process.

About Company: The company operates in the financial technology industry, providing digital solutions that simplify and streamline international payment processes for businesses and professionals.

Job Description:

1. Monthly Close & Accounting Governance:
• Own and drive the monthly close calendar for India and global entities
• Review outsourced accounting entries for accuracy, completeness, and cut-off
• Ensure adherence to close timelines; escalate delays or gaps proactively
• Prepare quarterly consolidated financial statements
• Manage Accrual vs Cash P&L
• Draft and review accounting memos for non-routine / judgmental items
• Ensure adherence to transfer pricing policies
• Manage multi-entity readiness including:
• Intercompany accounting & reconciliations
• Cost allocations and cross-charges
• Consolidation preparedness (data, mappings, eliminations)
2. Reconciliations & Transaction Controls:
• Setting up the reconciliations process and own the outcome
• Track and resolve reconciling items within defined SLAs
3. Statutory, Tax & Regulatory Compliances:
• Own end-to-end compliances across India and global entities
• Ensure adherence to Tax laws, Companies Act, and payments regulations (PA-CB)
• Maintain and track a centralized compliance calendar across jurisdictions
• Ensure timely filings with zero compliance lapses or penalties
4. Audit Readiness & External Audit Management:
• Maintain continuous audit readiness (not just year-end)
• Act as the primary SPOC for auditors and drive timely closure of audit queries
5. Internal Controls & Risk Management:
• Strengthen and enforce internal control frameworks
• Support IFC/RCM documentation and testing readiness
• Drive process improvements across payments, vendors, revenue, BRS, etc.
6. Outsourced Accounting & Vendor Management:
• Coordinate with external accounting firms across geographies
• Enforce SLAs on timeliness, quality, and documentation hygiene
• Drive standardization and continuous improvement in outputs
7. Process Building & Automation:
• Design and implement scalable finance processes and internal control frameworks to support multi-entity, cross-border operations and rapid business growth
• Drive automation across accounting and finance workflows (month-end close, reconciliations, reporting, AP/AR) to improve accuracy, reduce manual dependency, and shorten closing timelines
• Evaluate, implement, and optimize finance systems and tools to enable real-time reporting, process standardization, and audit-ready documentation
8. Financial Reporting & MIS:
• Prepare monthly MIS, variance analysis, and management reporting packs
• Support budgeting and forecasting processes with data accuracy and insights
• Analyze revenue, costs, and unit economics to highlight financial risks and opportunities
9. Cash Flow & Treasury Support:
• Assist in cash flow forecasting and working capital management
• Monitor liquidity across entities and optimize fund movements
• Support banking relationships and treasury documentation

Qualifications & Experience:
• Qualified Chartered Accountant (CA)
• 4–6 years of post-qualification experience in controllership, accounting, or audit
• Fintech/payments industry experience preferred
• Strong ownership of month-end close, reconciliations, and audit readiness
• Experience working with outsourced accounting teams is a strong plus
• Comfortable with Zoho Books/Xero/ERP systems
• Experience in fast-growing startup environments is a plus

Ideal Candidate Profile
• Prefers review, reconciliation, validation, and sign-off over data entry
• Operates with strong close discipline: checklists, timelines, and exception tracking
• High-integrity operator who can push back on weak documentation or approvals
• Process-oriented with strong control and audit mindset
• Comfortable with fintech/payment complexities (settlements, multi-bank flows, regulatory expectations)
• Capable of managing a team and ensuring timely, accurate finance outcome

Job Summary:

A hands-on senior accounting personnel to manage the outsourced finance and accounting functions for our entity in India. In this role, he/she will be responsible for working closely with our outsourcing partner, Genpact to ensure all transactional activities and treasury related tasks are accurately and efficiently performed. He/She will also be responsible for managing the local regulatory reporting and other statutory reporting and foreign currency reporting. He/She will report to the Associate Director, Controller Asia and Greater China and will work closely with cross-functional teams.

Key Responsibilities:

Financial Operations and Controllership
• Oversee day-to-day finance and accounting operations for the assigned entities, including managing outsourced service providers to ensure timely and accurate processing of transactional accounting activities.
• Provide overall controllership oversight, ensuring financial accuracy, integrity, and compliance with company policies and regulatory requirements.
• Manage the Record-to-Report (RTR) process, including General Ledger management, period-end close activities, and balance sheet reconciliations through BlackLine.
• Ensure completeness, accuracy, and timeliness of financial records and proper documentation of all financial transactions.

Financial Reporting
• Manage month-end and year-end close processes and ensure financial reporting is prepared in accordance with US GAAP, Group reporting standards, and local GAAP requirements.
• Prepare and review monthly financial statements, including P&L and Balance Sheet reporting, and provide financial analysis and insights to management.
• Monitor and analyze financial data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for improvement.

Treasury and Cash Management
• Manage treasury functions including cash management, bank relationships, and cash flow forecasting.
• Oversee foreign currency reporting and treasury-related activities.
• Manage banking operations and resolve banking-related matters.

Order-to-Cash and Working Capital Management
• Oversee Invoice-to-Cash (I2C) related processes including invoicing, customer credit management, collections, and accounts receivable.
• Support revenue recognition processes and ensure compliance with applicable accounting standards.
• Support working capital optimization through effective cash collection and cost control.

Procure-to-Pay and Operational Finance
• Oversee Accounts Payable and T&E processes, including vendor payments and expense reimbursements.
• Ensure proper management of invoicing, payments, and intercompany transactions.

Tax and Statutory Compliance
• Ensure compliance with all local statutory and regulatory reporting requirements in India, including timely filing of statutory returns.
• Support Tax payments and Audit support in coordination with TAX department.
• Work closely with the Asia Tax team on tax compliance matters.

Audit and Internal Controls
• Ensure compliance with SOX requirements and internal control frameworks, maintaining a strong and effective control environment.
• Coordinate and support internal audits, SOX audits, and external audits (e.g., EY).
• Ensure adherence to Delegation of Authority (DOA) and Segregation of Duties (SOD) requirements.
• Lead remediation efforts for any control gaps identified during internal or external audits.

Systems and Process Improvement
• Support SAP regression and integration testing related to finance system changes.
• Drive process improvements, standardization, and automation initiatives within finance operations.
• Ensure compliance with finance policies, governance standards, and data integrity requirements.

Stakeholder and other support
• Manage relationships with outsourced finance service providers, ensuring service quality and timely resolution of operational issues.
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure accurate and timely completion of financial reporting and operational activities.
• Prepare ad-hoc financial reports, analysis, and management presentations as required.
• Support business initiatives and other finance-related projects as assigned.

Hiring DGM-Accounting COE- IFRS 9/IFRS 15/ IFRS 16 with VOIS (Vodafone Intelligent Solutions)

About Job

Experience level: 8-16years

Location: Pune, Hybrid

Primary Skillset: Implementation experience in IFRS 9/IFRS 15/ IFRS

Qualification Required: CA/CMA/MBA in Finance

Job Description:

About this Role

The DGM – Accounting Centre of Excellence (IFRS 9 / IFRS 15 / IFRS 16) is a pivotal role within Vodafone’s Accounting Competence Centre, operating under the Group’s End‑to‑End Accounting & Reporting framework.

The individual provides strategic accounting leadership, ensures compliance with IFRS standards, and acts as a central governance authority supporting Vodafone’s markets globally.

As part of Lean Corporate Services (LCS), the Accounting CoE is responsible for driving standardisation, automation and high‑quality reporting across Vodafone Group. The role contributes directly to shaping, governing and continually improving global Record‑to‑Report (RTR) processes.

Who are you

You are a technically strong and collaborative accounting professional with deep expertise in IFRS 9, IFRS 15 and IFRS 16. You enjoy working in complex, cross‑functional environments and excel at guiding stakeholders through accounting judgement, governance and assurance.

You combine analytical skills with strong communication abilities, and you take pride in enabling high‑quality, compliant and timely financial reporting across multiple markets.

What you will do

Accounting Centre of Excellence & Governance
• Provide functional guidance and ensure consistent adherence to IFRS 9, IFRS 15 and IFRS 16 across markets.
• Lead accounting projects with detailed understanding of Vodafone‑specific commercial scenarios, contractual arrangements and accounting outcomes.
• Collaborate with Group Financial Reporting and Accounting Principles & Advisory teams to ensure alignment of policy, judgement and application.
• Support local Chief Accountants in delivering accurate financial statements, disclosures and management reporting under IFRS or local GAAP.
• Partner with RTR solutions and transformation teams to drive strategic accounting initiatives globally.
• Provide expertise for major commercial, transformation and VOIS process improvement projects.
• Strengthen and evolve global RTR frameworks, including Balance Sheet Reconciliations, Revenue, Lease and Accrual Governance.

Key Accountabilities
• Act as the CoE subject‑matter specialist for IFRS 9 / IFRS 15 / IFRS 16 across cross‑functional programmes.
• Own accounting considerations from design to implementation for transformation initiatives.
• Propose efficient, compliant and scalable accounting solutions tailored to business needs.
• Translate business requirements into actionable accounting and reporting outcomes.
• Maintain oversight of process performance, quality gaps and opportunities for continuous improvement.
• Drive automation, standardisation and control enhancements across IFRS processes.
• Maintain strong governance frameworks supporting accurate and compliant reporting.

What skills you need

Core Competencies, Knowledge & Experience
• Expert understanding of IFRS 9, IFRS 15 and IFRS 16, including application to complex commercial and financial structures.
• Comprehensive knowledge of revenue recognition, financial instruments and lease accounting.
• Experience in large, dynamic and matrixed organisations.
• Strong technical accounting judgement and problem‑solving skills.
• Excellent communication abilities to explain complex concepts clearly.
• A collaborative and customer‑focused mindset.

Must‑Have Technical / Professional Qualifications
• Chartered Accountant (or equivalent).
• Minimum 3 years’ hands‑on experience with IFRS 9 / IFRS 15 / IFRS 16.
• Demonstrated experience in finance transformation projects.
• Strong IT and data analytics literacy.
• Understanding of the telecommunications industry.
• Skilled in coordinating stakeholders and delivering high‑quality outcomes.
• Analytical, adaptable and collaborative approach with strong prioritisation abilities.

Key Performance Indicators
• Clean audit results with no material issues.
• On‑time and high‑quality Income Statement and Balance Sheet Reviews across markets.
• Strong governance and effective collaboration with internal and external stakeholders.

What skills you will learn
• Advanced application of IFRS across diverse commercial scenarios.
• Exposure to large‑scale global transformation programmes.
• Enhanced data and automation literacy within finance processes.
• Broader strategic understanding of Vodafone’s global markets and governance frameworks.
• Cross‑functional leadership in a complex, multinational environment.

About VOIS

VOIS (Vodafone Intelligent Solutions) is a strategic arm of Vodafone Group Plc, creating value and enhancing quality and efficiency across 28 countries, and operating from 7 locations: Albania, Egypt, Hungary, India, Romania, Spain and the UK.

Over 29,000 highly skilled individuals are dedicated to being Vodafone Group’s partner of choice for talent, technology, and transformation. We deliver the best services across IT, Business Intelligence Services, Customer Operations, Business Operations, HR, Finance, Supply Chain, HR Operations, and many more.

Established in 2006, VOIS has evolved into a global, multi-functional organisation, a Centre of Excellence for Intelligent Solutions focused on adding value and delivering business outcomes for Vodafone.

#VOIS #BeUnrivalled #CreateTheFuture

About Vodafone

Vodafone is a leading telecommunications company across Europe and Africa, continuously investing in advanced technologies to improve lives globally. As part of the Vodafone family—whether Vodafone, Vodacom, or VOIS—you will contribute to a culture of innovation, inclusion, and purpose. We value equality of opportunity and celebrate diversity in all forms. We maintain a workplace free from discrimination based on race, colour, age, gender identification, sexual orientation, disability, religion, nationality, and more.

The Senior Financial Analyst is responsible for providing business performance analysis, reporting, and forecasting. If you’re looking for a place where your work can be personally and professionally rewarding, don’t just join a company with a mission. Join a mission with a company behind it.

What You’ll Be Doing:

Job Description: Senior Analyst, Strategic Finance

Position Overview: We are seeking a Senior Analyst for our Strategic Finance team to support the Product and Technology Business Line. This role will support the company's financial planning, forecasting, budgeting, and reporting functions. The Senior FP&A Analyst will work closely with Accounting, Procurement, and department heads to provide insights and financial analysis that drive business decisions. The ideal candidate will have experience in managing monthly accruals, accounting processes, procurement processes, and will possess strong analytical and problem-solving abilities to drive business performance.

Responsibilities:

Support the Finance team by executing key deliverables below with excellent attention to detail:

· Work closely with the accounting team to ensure proper documentation and recording of accruals, journal entries, and other financial transactions (e.g., capital project codes, spend category, cost center, etc.).
• Provide financial oversight of procurement activities and ensure proper accounting treatment of purchasing and vendor transactions
• Maintain and update existing standard models (e.g., staffing, detailed budget builds, etc.) as well as contribute to further development of those models related to new initiatives (e.g., new strategic initiatives, staff versus contractor evaluation, CapEx strategy, etc.)
• Manage the monthly accrual process, ensuring accurate recognition of expenses in accordance with accounting principles and company policies.

· Assist with month-end and year-end closing activities to ensure timely and accurate financial reporting.
• Support monthly, quarterly, and annual reporting and budgeting cycles with KPIs, and insights tailored to Technology and Product leadership. Including: developing cost center budgets, capital planning, and project-level forecasting for Technology investments and Product initiatives.
• Track department-level performance (supporting business line leaders with monthly reports headcount, spend, variance analysis, department P&L forecasting)
• Maintain, create, automate, and improve Adaptive Insights financial models and reporting templates, focused on the evolving needs of the Technology and Product business area
• Liaise with other teams under the Finance umbrella (e.g., Accounting) to maintain accurate forecasting and partner on month-end variance analyses to better refine monthly forecasting
• Support special projects, including ROI analysis for new Technology investments, Product launches, and platform enhancements.

The Experience We Prefer:
• Chartered Accountant/CPA, CMA or Master’s degree in Business Finance or equivalent experience
• 3-5 years of experience as an Analyst or Associate in corporate finance (FP&A), investment banking, or other Finance role with financial modeling experience and ideally coming from an institution with a training / rotational / development program
• Proactive problem-solving skills with the ability to work independently
• Ability to take initiative while managing time across multiple workstreams and stakeholders
• Experience working with Accounts Payable, Accounting, and Procurement teams for invoice, Purchase Order, and accrual related items
• Extensive experience using Microsoft Excel
• Strong analytical abilities, with a track record of identifying insights from financial information and translating into business analytics and recommendations with a high level of accuracy
• Experience with ERP systems (e.g., Workday, SAP, Oracle) and FP&A tools (e.g., Hyperion, Adaptive Insights, Office Connect) is preferred.

To ensure a secure hiring process we have implemented several identity verification steps, including submission of a government issued photo ID. We conduct identity verification during interviews, and final interviews may require onsite attendance. All candidates must complete a comprehensive background check, in-person I-9 verification, and may be subject to drug screening prior to employment. The use of artificial intelligence tools during interviews is prohibited and monitored. Misrepresentation will result in immediate disqualification from consideration.

Mandatory Requirements:

Employees must have a high-speed broadband internet connection with a minimum speed of 50 Mbps and the ability to set up a wired connection to their home network to ensure effective remote work. These requirements may be updated as needed by the business.

​

Senior Level

Job Description:

Ecolab is seeking an FP&A Director responsible for leading financial planning and analysis activities across the organization. This role serves as a key point of contact for Division Finance Managers and enables them to drive best‑in‑class financial performance and business insights. The position requires strong partnerships across multiple stakeholders and offers significant exposure and growth opportunities. As FP&A Director, you will work closely with divisional finance teams to drive results and strengthen financial rigor.

What’s in it for You?
• Join a fast-growing organization offering a competitive salary and benefits.
• Influence and shape your career with a company committed to continuous growth.
• Work in an environment that values diverse people, perspectives, and ideas.
• Feel proud to contribute to a company that ensures clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments.

What You Will Do

• Lead and coordinate the development of annual plans, ensuring strong collaboration among all key stakeholders.
• Oversee monthly close and forecast processes—including providing direction, reviewing results, supporting system loads, and preparing content for senior leadership business reviews.

• Collaborate with broader finance and business teams to support critical reporting needs and enhance reporting capabilities and tools.

• Proficiency in BI tools such as Tableau or Power BI

• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels

Responsibilities

Lead Analyst - Accounting

Exp - 6 - 10 years

Shift Timings - 1.30 PM - 10.30 PM
• Build, maintain, and enhance Power BI dashboards to support forecasting, performance tracking, and management reporting for SCBUs.
• Design intuitive, leadership‑friendly visualizations that highlight KPIs, financial trends, and business drivers.
• Automate manual reports and recurring processes using Power BI, Power Query, and other analytical tools to improve efficiency and reduce turnaround time.
• Collect, clean, aggregate, and validate data from multiple SCBUs and enterprise systems to ensure accuracy and reliability of reporting.
• Establish and maintain data links between different sources, building data models, and maintaining reporting datasets to support end‑to‑end dashboard operations. (Power BI datasets, Oracle Essbase/ Peoplesoft, Advanced Excel, PowerPoint, etc.) to create a seamless reporting ecosystem.
• Support forecasting, actuals reporting, and value analysis by preparing data inputs and performing first‑level variance reviews.
• Provide initial analytical support for performance metrics, and operational KPIs, highlighting anomalies and key insights.
• Assist in documenting processes, data flows, and dashboard refresh cycles.
• Highlight trends, outliers, and data inconsistencies.
• Support the SCBU FP&A team in process redesign, reporting improvements, and system enhancements to align with evolving business requirements.
• Work collaboratively with SCBU FP&A teams and cross‑BU finance partners to share best practices and ensure alignment of data and reporting standards.
• Gain a strong understanding of financial data structures, systems, and reporting tools used within the organization.
• Manage assigned deliverables independently, including prioritizing multiple tasks, meeting deadlines, and maintaining high‑quality outputs.
• Prepare clear, visually effective presentations and insights for leadership consumption.

Qualifications

Education & Experience
• Master’s degree / Professional Degree / Relevant Industry Certification
• Preferably Chartered Accountant or MBA Finance
• 6-10 years’ of relevant experience in handling complex datasets, analytics, reporting, automation & PowerBI skills. (preferably in BFSI Industry, either in GCC / service provider environment)

Technical Skills
• Strong hands‑on experience with Power BI—data modeling, DAX, dashboards, and report publishing.
• Proficiency in Oracle Essbase/ Peoplesoft, Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables, Power Query), PowerPoint.

Analytical & Soft Skills

• Ability to work independently, manage multiple tasks, and prioritize effectively.
• Eagerness to learn and develop deeper financial acumen.

Job Description

As the world works and lives faster, FIS is leading the way. Our fintech solutions touch nearly every market, company and person on the planet. Our teams are inclusive and diverse. Our colleagues work together and celebrate together. If you want to advance the world of fintech, we’d like to ask you~ Are you FIS?

About The Role

The Business Analyst will work as a liaison between the product management and development team to analyze the business requirements, convert those into functional specifications and get projects ready for development. The candidate will also need to participate in integration testing and validating new functions to certify they meet business requirements. The role also involves working with the SQA team & the Release Engineering team to make sure projects are released to clients on time. The candidate needs to possess strong analytical skills, excellent communication abilities, and a deep understanding of business operations and the overall SDLC processes.

What You Will Be Doing
• Analyzing Business requirement

• Functional/Integration testing
• Creating and maintaining project artifacts
• Project Implementation in co-ordination with SQA and Release Engineering

• Understanding of risk monitoring and margining methods

• Knowledge of Agile/Scrum practices
• Knowhow of SDLC processes

What We Offer You

Prior experience as a Business Analyst involved in the US trading/margining systems is preferred.

Recruitment at FIS works primarily on a direct sourcing model; a relatively small portion of our hiring is through recruitment agencies.
